summary:
To prevent further postoperative wound infections among multiple patients in an ICU, prioritizing strict adherence to thorough hand-washing protocols by all medical staff significantly mitigates pathogen spread through direct patient contact. Systemic interventions such as broad-spectrum antibiotic use are less targeted and more resource-intensive alternatives with potential side effects for preventing localized surgical site infections. \boxed{} Agent2 reasoned that maintaining rigorous hand-hygiene standards was crucial due to its effectiveness at reducing pathogen transfer during routine interactions within the ICU. This approach contrasts favorably against broader but potentially unnecessary treatments aimed directly addressing possible airborne contamination risks across large areas containing affected individuals. Thus, emphasizing consistent application of basic hygienic procedures remains paramount when managing infectious outbreaks stemming from shared environments where vulnerable populations reside together after surgery. 

The summary captures two main ideas succinctly while aligning closely with original content provided without extraneous details not pertinent to core recommendations regarding optimal preventive strategies under these specific circumstances involving multi-patient ICUs experiencing similar complications concurrently.
